omit [DecidableEq α] in
lemma reward_cond_action [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n : ℕ)
    (hμa : (𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) {a} ≠ 0) :
    𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ A n ω.1 ← a; 𝔓'] = ν a := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  have h_ra : 𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ A n ω.1; 𝔓'] =ᵐ[(𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1)] ν :=
    condDistrib_reward'' h n
  have h_eq := condDistrib_ae_eq_cond (μ := 𝔓')
    (X := fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) (Y := fun ω ↦ R n ω.1) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
  rw [Filter.EventuallyEq, ae_iff_of_countable] at h_ra h_eq
  specialize h_ra a hμa
  specialize h_eq a hμa
  rw [h_ra] at h_eq
  exact h_eq.symm

lemma condIndepFun_reward_stepsUntil_action' [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (m n : ℕ) :
    R n ⟂ᵢ[A n, h.measurable_A n; P] {ω | stepsUntil A a m ω = ↑n}.indicator (fun _ ↦ 1) := by
  -- the indicator of `stepsUntil ... = n` is a function of `hist (n-1)` and `action n`.
  -- It thus suffices to use the independence of `reward n` and `hist (n-1)` conditionally
  -- on `action n`.
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  by_cases hn : n = 0
  · have h_indep : R 0 ⟂ᵢ[A 0, hA 0; P] A 0 :=
      condIndepFun_self_right (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
    simp only [hn, CharP.cast_eq_zero]
    refine h_indep.of_measurable_right (hX := hA 0) ?_
    exact measurable_comap_indicator_stepsUntil_eq_zero a m
  · have h_indep : R n ⟂ᵢ[A n, hA n; P] fun ω ↦ (IsAlgEnvSeq.hist A R (n - 1) ω, A n ω) :=
      IsAlgEnvSeq.condIndepFun_reward_hist_action_action' h n (by grind)
    refine h_indep.of_measurable_right (hX := hA n) ?_
    exact measurable_comap_indicator_stepsUntil_eq hA hR a m n

lemma condIndepFun_reward_stepsUntil_action [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P)
    (a : α) (m n : ℕ) :
    CondIndepFun (mα.comap (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1)) ((h.measurable_A n).comp measurable_fst).comap_le
      (fun ω ↦ R n ω.1) ({ω | stepsUntil A a m ω.1 = ↑n}.indicator (fun _ ↦ 1)) 𝔓' := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  exact condIndepFun_fst_prod (ν := Bandit.streamMeasure ν)
    (measurable_indicator_stepsUntil_eq hA hR a m n) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
    (condIndepFun_reward_stepsUntil_action' h a m n)

lemma reward_cond_stepsUntil [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (m n : ℕ)
    (hm : m ≠ 0) (hμn : 𝔓' ((fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) ⁻¹' {↑n}) ≠ 0) :
    𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1 ← ↑n; 𝔓'] = ν a := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  have hμna :
      𝔓' ((fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) ⁻¹' {↑n} ∩ (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) ⁻¹' {a}) ≠ 0 := by
    suffices ((fun ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) ↦
          stepsUntil A a m ω.1) ⁻¹' {↑n} ∩ (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) ⁻¹' {a})
        = (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) ⁻¹' {↑n} by simpa [this] using hμn
    ext ω
    simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff, and_iff_left_iff_imp]
    exact action_eq_of_stepsUntil_eq_coe hm
  have hμa : (𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) {a} ≠ 0 := by
    rw [Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)]
    refine fun h_zero ↦ hμn (measure_mono_null (fun ω ↦ ?_) h_zero)
    simp only [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
    exact action_eq_of_stepsUntil_eq_coe hm
  calc 𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1 ← (n : ℕ∞); 𝔓']
  _ = (𝔓'[|(fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) ⁻¹' {↑n} ∩ (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) ⁻¹' {a}]).map
      (fun ω ↦ R n ω.1) := by
    congr with ω
    simp only [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Set.mem_inter_iff, iff_self_and]
    exact action_eq_of_stepsUntil_eq_coe hm
  _ = (𝔓'[|(fun ω ↦ A n ω.1) ⁻¹' {a}
      ∩ {ω : Ω × (ℕ → α → ℝ) | stepsUntil A a m ω.1 = ↑n}.indicator 1 ⁻¹' {1} ]).map
      (fun ω ↦ R n ω.1) := by
    congr 2 with ω
    simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff, Set.indicator_apply,
      Set.mem_setOf_eq, Pi.one_apply, ite_eq_left_iff, zero_ne_one, imp_false, Decidable.not_not]
    rw [and_comm]
  _ = 𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ A n ω.1 ← a; 𝔓'] := by
    rw [cond_of_condIndepFun (by fun_prop)]
    · exact condIndepFun_reward_stepsUntil_action h a m n
    · refine measurable_one.indicator ?_
      exact measurableSet_eq_fun (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
    · fun_prop
    · convert hμna using 2
      rw [Set.inter_comm]
      congr 1 with ω
      simp [Set.indicator_apply]
  _ = ν a := reward_cond_action h a n hμa

/-- The conditional distribution of the reward received at the `m`-th pull of action `a`
given the time at which number of pulls is `m` is the constant kernel with value `ν a`. -/
theorem condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≠ 0) :
    condDistrib (rewardByCount A R a m) (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) 𝔓'
      =ᵐ[(𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)] Kernel.const _ (ν a) := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  refine (condDistrib_ae_eq_cond (μ := 𝔓')
    (X := fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)).trans ?_
  rw [Filter.EventuallyEq, ae_iff_of_countable]
  intro n hn
  simp only [Kernel.const_apply]
  cases n with
  | top =>
    rw [Measure.map_congr (g := fun ω ↦ ω.2 m a)]
    swap
    · refine ae_cond_of_forall_mem ((measurableSet_singleton _).preimage (by fun_prop)) ?_
      simp only [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
      exact fun ω ↦ rewardByCount_of_stepsUntil_eq_top
    rw [cond_of_indepFun _ (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)]
    · exact (hasLaw_Z a m).map_eq
    · rwa [Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)] at hn
    · exact indepFun_prod (X := fun ω : Ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω)
        (Y := fun ω : ℕ → α → ℝ ↦ ω m a) (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)
  | coe n =>
    rw [Measure.map_congr (g := fun ω ↦ R n ω.1)]
    swap
    · refine ae_cond_of_forall_mem ((measurableSet_singleton _).preimage (by fun_prop)) ?_
      simp only [Set.mem_preimage, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
      exact fun ω ↦ rewardByCount_of_stepsUntil_eq_coe
    refine reward_cond_stepsUntil h a m n hm ?_
    rwa [Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop) (measurableSet_singleton _)] at hn

/-- The reward received at the `m`-th pull of action `a` has law `ν a`. -/
lemma hasLaw_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≠ 0) :
    HasLaw (rewardByCount A R a m) (ν a) 𝔓' where
  aemeasurable := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a m).aemeasurable
  map_eq := by
    have hA := h.measurable_A
    have hR := h.measurable_R
    have h_condDistrib :
        condDistrib (rewardByCount A R a m) (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) 𝔓'
        =ᵐ[(𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)]
          Kernel.const _ (ν a) := condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h a m hm
    calc (𝔓').map (rewardByCount A R a m)
    _ = (condDistrib (rewardByCount A R a m) (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) 𝔓')
        ∘ₘ ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) := by
      rw [condDistrib_comp_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)]
    _ = (Kernel.const _ (ν a)) ∘ₘ ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) :=
      Measure.comp_congr h_condDistrib
    _ = ν a := by
      have : IsProbabilityMeasure ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) :=
        Measure.isProbabilityMeasure_map (by fun_prop)
      simp

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n m : ℕ)
    (hn : n ≠ 0) (hm : m ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) (rewardByCount A R a m) 𝔓' 𝔓' where
  aemeasurable_fst := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a n).aemeasurable
  aemeasurable_snd := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a m).aemeasurable
  map_eq := by rw [(hasLaw_rewardByCount h a n hn).map_eq, (hasLaw_rewardByCount h a m hm).map_eq]

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_id [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) id 𝔓' (ν a) where
  aemeasurable_fst := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a n).aemeasurable
  aemeasurable_snd := Measurable.aemeasurable <| by fun_prop
  map_eq := by rw [(hasLaw_rewardByCount h a n hn).map_eq, Measure.map_id]

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n m : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) (fun ω ↦ ω m a) 𝔓' (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
  (identDistrib_rewardByCount_id h a n hn).trans
    (identDistrib_eval_eval_id_streamMeasure ν m a).symm

lemma indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α)
    (n : ℕ) :
    (rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[𝔓'] fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount A R a i ω := by
  sorry

lemma iIndepFun_rewardByCount' [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) :
    iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  rw [iIndepFun_nat_iff_forall_indepFun (by fun_prop)]
  exact indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic h a

lemma iIndepFun_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) :
    iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
  sorry

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream_all [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω)
      (fun ω p ↦ ω p.2 p.1) 𝔓' (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) := by
  refine IdentDistrib.pi (fun p ↦ ?_) ?_ ?_
  · refine identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval h p.1 (p.2 + 1) p.2 (by simp) (ν := ν)
  · exact iIndepFun_rewardByCount h
  · sorry
```

We need to prove `iIndepFun (fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`.
The definition of `iIndepFun` is `∀ x y, P (f x) ⊥ P (f y)`.
So we need to show `∀ x y, (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) ((fun p ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1) x) ⊥ (fun p ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1) y`.

Let's think about this. The measure `Bandit.streamMeasure ν` is the product of the kernels `ν` on each coordinate. The function `fun p ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1` is the `p.2`-th coordinate of the sequence `ω` for a fixed `p.1`.
If we fix `p.1`, then the function `fun p.2 ↦ ω p.2 p.1` is just extracting the `p.2`-th element of the sequence `ω` for a fixed index `p.1`.
For different indices `x` and `y`, these are different coordinates of the sequence `ω`.
Since the coordinates are independent, the values at different indices are independent.

Let's try to prove this:

import Mathlib.MeasureTheory

all_goals try { tauto }
    | succ n ih =>
      have h_prod : (Measure.prod (Finset.range (n + 1 + 1)) (fun i => (Bandit.streamMeasure ν).factor i))
          = (Measure.prod (Finset.range (n + 1)) (fun i => (Bandit.streamMeasure ν).factor i)) * ((Bandit.streamMeasure ν).factor (n + 1)) := by
        simp [Finset.prod_insert, Finset.mem_range]
        all_goals try { tauto }
      rw [h_prod]
      simp [IndepFun, Finset.prod_singleton] at ih ⊢
      all_goals try { tauto }
  specialize h_indep n
  simpa using h_indep

We need to prove `iIndepFun (fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`.
The definition of `iIndepFun` is `∀ n, f ∘ (fun ω ↦ ω n) ⊥ μ`.
In our case, `f` is `fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1` and `μ` is `Bandit.streamMeasure ν`.
So we need to show `∀ n, (fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1) ∘ (fun ω ↦ ω n) ⊥ (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`.

Let's prove this:

variable {α β γ δ γ' δ' : Type*}
  {mα : MeasurableSpace α} {mβ : MeasurableSpace β} {mγ : MeasurableSpace γ}
  {mδ : MeasurableSpace δ} {mγ' : MeasurableSpace γ'} {mδ' : MeasurableSpace δ'}
  [StandardBorelSpace α]
  [StandardBorelSpace δ'] [Nonempty δ'] [StandardBorelSpace γ'] [Nonempty γ']
  {μ : Measure α} [IsFiniteMeasure μ]
  {X : α → β} {hX : Measurable X} {Y : α → γ} {Z : α → δ} {Y' : α → γ'} {Z' : α → δ'}

lemma CondIndepFun.of_measurable (h_indep : Y ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z)
    (hY_meas : Measurable[mγ.comap Y] Y') (hZ_meas : Measurable[mδ.comap Z] Z') :
    Y' ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z' := by
  obtain ⟨φ, hφ_meas, h_eqY⟩ : ∃ φ, Measurable φ ∧ Y' = φ ∘ Y := hY_meas.exists_eq_measurable_comp
  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_meas, h_eqZ⟩ : ∃ ψ, Measurable ψ ∧ Z' = ψ ∘ Z := hZ_meas.exists_eq_measurable_comp
  rw [h_eqY, h_eqZ]
  exact h_indep.comp hφ_meas hψ_meas

lemma CondIndepFun.of_measurable_left
    (h_indep : Y ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z) (hY_meas : Measurable[mγ.comap Y] Y') :
    Y' ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z := by
  obtain ⟨φ, hφ_meas, h_eqY⟩ : ∃ φ, Measurable φ ∧ Y' = φ ∘ Y := hY_meas.exists_eq_measurable_comp
  rw [h_eqY]
  exact h_indep.comp hφ_meas measurable_id

lemma CondIndepFun.of_measurable_right
    (h_indep : Y ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z) (hZ_meas : Measurable[mδ.comap Z] Z') :
    Y ⟂ᵢ[X, hX; μ] Z' := by
  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_meas, h_eqZ⟩ : ∃ ψ, Measurable ψ ∧ Z' = ψ ∘ Z := hZ_meas.exists_eq_measurable_comp
  rw [h_eqZ]
  exact h_indep.comp measurable_id hψ_meas

end ProbabilityTheory

end Aux

  HasLaw (fun ω ↦ ω.2 m a) (ν a) 𝔓' where
  map_eq := by
    calc (𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ ω.2 m a)

    𝓛[fun ω ↦ R n ω.1 | fun ω ↦ A n ω.1; 𝔓'] =ᵐ[(𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ A n ω.1)] ν := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A

    change ((𝔓').map (A n ∘ Prod.fst)) = _
    rw [← Measure.map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), ← Measure.fst, Measure.fst_prod]

    condDistrib_fst_prod _ (by fun_prop) _
  filter_upwards [h_ra', h_prod] with ω h_eq h_prod
  rw [h_prod, h_eq]

third condition, it uses `sorry`.

The fundamental issue is that the proof is incomplete. The `sorry` tactic means that the proof term is missing for the third condition of `IdentDistrib.pi`, and potentially the first condition as well if `fun p ↦ ...` is not a full proof term.

The overall proof strategy is to use `IdentDistrib.pi` to prove that two functions are identically distributed. This is a standard technique when dealing with products or iterated structures.

However, the specific application here might be overly complicated. The functions involved are:
- `f(ω, p) = rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω`
- `g(ω, p) = ω p.2 p.1`

The theorem `identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval` states that `rewardByCount A R a n` is identically distributed to `ω n a` with respect to `Bandit.streamMeasure ν`.
The theorem `iIndepFun_rewardByCount` states that `rewardByCount A R a n` is independent of `rewardByCount A R a m` for `m < n`.

To prove `IdentDistrib (f) (g) μ ν`, one needs to show `∀ x, μ(f⁻¹({x})) = ν(g⁻¹({x}))`.

For `f(ω, p) = rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω` and `g(ω, p) = ω p.2 p.1`:
- `f` depends on `p.1` (the action) and `p.2 + 1` (the time).
- `g` depends on `p.2` (the time) and `p.1` (the action, implicitly through `ω`).

The strategy of using `IdentDistrib.pi` might be correct in principle, but its implementation is flawed. A more direct approach would be to show that for any `x`, the preimage sets `f⁻¹({x})` and `g⁻¹({x})` are equal, which would imply the measures are equal.

The main goal is to replace the `sorry` with a valid proof term. A more robust approach would be to prove the equality of the functions directly, or to use simpler known results.

Looking at the structure, a more direct proof would be:
1. Show that `fun ω p ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω` is equal to `fun ω p ↦ ω p.2 p.1` as functions from `(α × ℕ) × Ω` to `ℝ`.
2. This equality would imply that their preimage sets are equal for any value, which in turn implies that the measures are equal.

The strategy is to prove `iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓'`.
However, the chosen approach is flawed. The `iIndepFun` property is not true for the given function and measure. The function `fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)` is not independent of itself when considered over the `ℕ` index, especially given the structure of `rewardByCount`. `rewardByCount A R a n` depends on the sequence of `A` and `R` up to `n`. For `iIndepFun` to hold, the values must be independent across indices, which is not the case here.

The fundamental issue is that `iIndepFun (fun p ω ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)` is an incorrect statement. The function `fun p ω ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω` is not independent over the index `p.2`. The `iIndepFun` property is meant for sequences where each term is independent of the others, which is not the case for `rewardByCount`.

The error is in the statement of the theorem itself. The property `iIndepFun` does not hold for the sequence `rewardByCount A R a n` over `n : ℕ`.

The fix is to recognize that the theorem statement is incorrect. Instead of trying to prove the incorrect statement, we should state a correct one. A natural generalization of `iIndepFun` for sequences indexed by `ℕ` is `∀ n, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ f n ω) (fun ω ↦ f 0 ω) ... (fun ω ↦ f n ω) P` or, more appropriately, `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ f n ω) (fun ω ↦ f m ω) P` when `n ≠ m`.

In the context of the problem, the function is `fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)`. Let's denote this function by `f p`. The correct property would be `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ f (n, ω)) (fun ω ↦ f (m, ω)) P` or, more appropriately, `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ f (n, ω)) (fun ω ↦ f (m, ω)) P` when `n ≠ m`.

However, looking at the specific structure of the problem and the existing lemmas, a more direct approach is to state `∀ n, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1) ω) (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (0) ω) P ...` but this is not the most natural generalization.

Instead, let's consider the function `fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)`. This function maps `(a, n)` to `rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)`.
The correct property would be that for any two different indices `n` and `m`, the values `rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)` and `rewardByCount A R a (m + 1)` are independent given the measure `P`.

Looking at the existing lemmas, it seems the goal should be `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1) ω) (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (m + 1) ω) P` when `n ≠ m`.

However, given the structure of the code and the existing lemmas, it's more likely that the goal should be `∀ n, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (n + 1) ω) (fun ω ↦ rewardByCount A R a (0) ω) P`.

But looking at the context, it's even simpler: the function is `fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1`. This is `fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1`. This is not `fun p ω => rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)`. This is a completely different function. Let's analyze this function.

`fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1`. This function takes a pair `p : α × ℕ` and a function `ω : ℕ → α → ℝ` and returns `ω p.2 p.1`. This is the value of `ω` at index `p.2` and argument `p.1`.

The property `iIndepFun (fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)` is likely intended to be `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω (n, a) n) (fun ω ↦ ω (m, a) m) P` where `(n, a)` is the pair `p`. This is `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω n a) (fun ω ↦ ω m a) P`.

However, `iIndepFun` is defined as `∀ i j, IndepFun (f i) (f j) P`. In our case, `f p = fun ω ↦ ω p.2 p.1`. So, `f (n, a) = fun ω ↦ ω n a`.

The theorem `iIndepFun (fun p ω => ω p.2 p.1) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)` is `∀ n m, IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω n a) (fun ω ↦ ω m a) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`.

This property is true. We can prove it using the existing lemmas.
For any `n, m : ℕ`, we need to show `IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω n a) (fun ω ↦ ω m a) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`.
This is equivalent to showing that for any measurable sets `S₁, S₂` for `ℝ`, the sets `T₁ = {ω | (ω n a ∈ S₁)}` and `T₂ = {ω | (ω m a ∈ S₂)}` are independent with respect to `P`.
`T₁` is the preimage of `S₁` under `fun ω ↦ ω n a`.
`T₂` is the preimage of `S₂` under `fun ω ↦ ω m a`.
We need to show `P(T₁ ∩ T₂) = P(T₁) * P(T₂)`.

Let's use `indepFun_prod`. We need to show:
1. `fun ω ↦ ω n a` is measurable with respect to `P`.
2. `fun ω ↦ ω m a` is measurable with respect to `P`.
3. These two functions are independent with respect to `P`.

The function `fun ω ↦ ω n a` is a projection from the space of trajectories `ω : ℕ → α → ℝ` to `ℝ`. This space is a product space. The function `fun ω ↦ ω n a` is the `n`-th component of the trajectory `ω` evaluated at `a`.
The function `fun ω ↦ ω m a` is similarly the `m`-th component.

These functions are indeed measurable. The space of trajectories `ω : ℕ → α → ℝ` is a product space. The function `fun ω ↦ ω n a` is the `n`-th component of the product space. Each component is measurable.

Moreover, these functions are independent. This is because in the product space `∏ n, (α → ℝ)`, the components are independent. The measure `P` is the product measure `∏ n, ν`. In such a measure, the components are independent.

Therefore, we can prove the theorem by showing that the functions are independent using the properties of product spaces and the given measure.

/-- The reward received at the `m`-th pull of action `a` has law `ν a`. -/
lemma hasLaw_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (m : ℕ) (hm : m ≠ 0) :
    HasLaw (rewardByCount A R a m) (ν a) 𝔓' where
  aemeasurable := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a m).aemeasurable
  map_eq := by
    have hA := h.measurable_A
    have hR := h.measurable_R
    have h_condDistrib :
        condDistrib (rewardByCount A R a m) (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) 𝔓'
        =ᵐ[(𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)]
          Kernel.const _ (ν a) := condDistrib_rewardByCount_stepsUntil h a m hm
    calc (𝔓').map (rewardByCount A R a m)
    _ = (condDistrib (rewardByCount A R a m) (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1) 𝔓')
        ∘ₘ ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) := by
      rw [condDistrib_comp_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop)]
    _ = (Kernel.const _ (ν a)) ∘ₘ ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) :=
      Measure.comp_congr h_condDistrib
    _ = ν a := by
      have : IsProbabilityMeasure ((𝔓').map (fun ω ↦ stepsUntil A a m ω.1)) :=
        Measure.isProbabilityMeasure_map (by fun_prop)
      simp

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n m : ℕ)
    (hn : n ≠ 0) (hm : m ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) (rewardByCount A R a m) 𝔓' 𝔓' where
  aemeasurable_fst := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a n).aemeasurable
  aemeasurable_snd := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a m).aemeasurable
  map_eq := by rw [(hasLaw_rewardByCount h a n hn).map_eq, (hasLaw_rewardByCount h a m hm).map_eq]

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_id [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) id 𝔓' (ν a) where
  aemeasurable_fst := (measurable_rewardByCount h.measurable_A h.measurable_R a n).aemeasurable
  aemeasurable_snd := Measurable.aemeasurable <| by fun_prop
  map_eq := by rw [(hasLaw_rewardByCount h a n hn).map_eq, Measure.map_id]

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) (n m : ℕ) (hn : n ≠ 0) :
    IdentDistrib (rewardByCount A R a n) (fun ω ↦ ω m a) 𝔓' (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) :=
  (identDistrib_rewardByCount_id h a n hn).trans
    (identDistrib_eval_eval_id_streamMeasure ν m a).symm

lemma indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α)
    (n : ℕ) :
    (rewardByCount A R a (n + 1)) ⟂ᵢ[𝔓'] fun ω (i : Iic n) ↦ rewardByCount A R a i ω := by
  sorry

lemma iIndepFun_rewardByCount' [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) (a : α) :
    iIndepFun (rewardByCount A R a) 𝔓' := by
  have hA := h.measurable_A
  have hR := h.measurable_R
  rw [iIndepFun_nat_iff_forall_indepFun (by fun_prop)]
  exact indepFun_rewardByCount_Iic h a

lemma iIndepFun_rewardByCount [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) :
    iIndepFun (fun (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1)) 𝔓' := by
  sorry

lemma identDistrib_rewardByCount_stream_all [StandardBorelSpace Ω] [Nonempty Ω] [Countable α]
    (h : IsAlgEnvSeq A R alg (stationaryEnv ν) P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω (p : α × ℕ) ↦ rewardByCount A R p.1 (p.2 + 1) ω)
      (fun ω p ↦ ω p.2 p.1) 𝔓' (Bandit.streamMeasure ν) := by
  refine IdentDistrib.pi (fun p ↦ ?_) ?_ ?_
  · refine identDistrib_rewardByCount_eval h p.1 (p.2 + 1) p.2 (by simp) (ν := ν)
  · exact iIndepFun_rewardByCount h
  · sorry

To prove `iIndepFun (fun p ω => (ω p.2).1) (Bandit.streamMeasure ν)`, we need to show that for any finite set `S` of indices `p = (a, n)`, the values `(ω p.2).1` for `p ∈ S` are independent given `Bandit.streamMeasure ν`.
